name: The Moral Machine Experiment
description: A massive cross-cultural study of human moral preferences: an online platform collected roughly 40 million decisions about unavoidable autonomous-vehicle dilemmas from millions of people across 233 countries and territories. The aggregated, openly released data reveal how moral judgments about whom to save vary across cultures — a behavioral resource for studying social and moral cognition at global scale.
citation: Awad E, Dsouza S, Kim R, et al. (2018). The Moral Machine experiment. Nature, 563(7729), 59-64.
